evals.prompt: build me a landing page for a coffee roastery called 'Ember & Ash'. they're premium, small-batch, wood-fire roasted. the vibe is warm, smoky, artisanal. it's a new project, just an empty directory with an index.html.
evals.expected_output: Commits to a specific aesthetic direction (e.g. warm/smoky/artisanal) before coding. Chooses distinctive fonts — NOT Inter, Roboto, Arial, or Space Grotesk. Builds a color palette from the brand identity (warm tones, earth, smoke) rather than purple gradients. Produces working HTML/CSS with intentional layout choices (not cookie-cutter card grids).
expectations: Articulates a design direction before writing code — purpose, tone, differentiation
Uses distinctive fonts, NOT banned defaults (Inter, Roboto, Arial, Space Grotesk, system fonts)
Color palette reflects the brand (warm/smoky) — no purple gradients on white

evals.prompt: i have a next.js app with tailwind. need a dashboard page at app/dashboard/page.tsx. it shows user's API usage: requests today, quota remaining, response time p50/p99, and a list of recent API calls. the existing app uses src/components/ for shared stuff and has a dark theme.
evals.expected_output: Detects the Next.js + Tailwind stack and existing patterns (src/components/, dark theme). Creates app/dashboard/page.tsx matching project conventions. Chooses a design direction appropriate for a developer dashboard (data-dense, functional). Uses CSS variables or Tailwind tokens. Layout breaks from generic card grids — uses the spatial strategy that fits the data.
expectations: Detects existing stack (Next.js, Tailwind, dark theme) and matches project conventions
Commits to a design direction before coding — articulates purpose/tone for a dev dashboard
Produces working code in the correct file location using project's styling approach

evals.prompt: make a 404 page for my portfolio site. it's vanilla html/css/js. the rest of the site has a brutalist aesthetic — monospace fonts, harsh contrast, raw html feel. make the 404 page match but be memorable.
evals.expected_output: Matches the existing brutalist aesthetic (monospace, harsh contrast, raw). Does NOT default to soft rounded cards or gradient backgrounds. The 404 page has a distinctive element that makes it memorable. Uses vanilla HTML/CSS/JS as specified. Verifies the result matches brutalist direction, not generic defaults.
expectations: Matches existing brutalist aesthetic — monospace, harsh contrast, raw feel
Includes a distinctive/memorable element specific to the 404 experience
Does NOT fall back to generic soft UI patterns (rounded cards, gradients, friendly illustrations)
